title: Bacopa neuwiedii
intro: Bacopa neuwiedii, also known as Bacopa monnieri, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to tropical and subtropical regions, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to enhance cognitive function and memory. The health benefits of Bacopa neuwiedii include improved memory, cognitive processing speed, and reduced stress levels, making it a popular natural remedy for age-related cognitive decline and anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Bacopa neuwiedii include bacosides, which are a group of saponin glycosides responsible for the plant's cognitive-enhancing effects, as well as other compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. Bacopa neuwiedii is commonly prepared as a capsule or tablet supplement, as well as a liquid extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its cognitive and nootropic effects.
taxonomy: Bacopa neuwiedii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Bacopa, which consists of various aquatic plants. Bacopa neuwiedii is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions. The plant is also known as Bacopa neuwiedii, Bacopa monnieri var. neuwiedii, and Ochrosia neuwiedii. Further classification may include the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida.
